title: Before The World Was Made
content:
If I make the lashes dark
And the eyes more bright
And the lips more scarlet,
Or ask if all be right
From mirror after mirror,
No vanity's displayed:
I'm looking for the face I had
Before the world was made.

What if I look upon a man
As though on my beloved,
And my blood be cold the while
And my heart unmoved?
Why should he think me cruel
Or that he is betrayed?
I'd have him love the thing that was
Before the world was made.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Butler Yeats delivers a powerful performance in "Before The World Was Made"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
